mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or forward returns are positive in >55% of rising rate periods over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.1) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, driving corporate earnings growth and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST yields negative 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity, forcing firms to cut margins and generating negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods produce positive 20-day returns in >55% of observations, or regime filter adds no predictive alpha.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL month-over-month increase above 0.4% triggers immediate inflation hedging and rate hike expectations, causing sharp short-term sell-offs in equities.
failure_condition: High inflation prints fail to generate negative 5-day returns in >65% of cases, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with a stable or declining unemployment rate confirms a healthy labor market, supporting consumer spending and positive quarterly equity performance.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal yields negative 60-day returns in >50% of instances, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from noise in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly translates to increased corporate revenues and profit margins, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid industrial production expansion during a post-shock recovery phase signals pent-up demand fulfillment, driving immediate risk-on flows and positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Strong INDPRO prints in recovery regimes fail to produce positive 5-day returns in >60% of cases, or the regime condition provides no incremental predictive power.
